item.title: American Memes Vol 2
item.description: I'm picking back up where I left off clearing out some of the memes I spent a frankly absurd amount of time organizing into folders on my phone. As always, I Hope you all enjoyed. I used to post all of the links to my past posts below but the ends of these posts were getting a bit unwieldy. So, at the risk of overcomplicating things I've made a few (sometimes pretty loosely) categorized repositories of my past posts if you'd like to see more I'll link to those below. Movies, TV, and More https://imgur.com/gallery/qWQKZr5 Books, Comics, and More https://imgur.com/gallery/0unBaDX Gaming, Tech, and More https://imgur.com/gallery/17tib1E Food, Family, and Animals https://imgur.com/gallery/zlsjOQa Work, Politics and America https://imgur.com/gallery/zQrWXQE ADHD, Autism, Mental Health, and Millenial https://imgur.com/gallery/G1E6yWd NSFW Memes https://imgur.com/gallery/2I9HXEp Reactions https://imgur.com/gallery/uDjMX0P
item.dc:creator: GeeeButtersnaps

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1441 (the glory of kettle chips)
item.description:
I wanted to save this for the 24th, but... I'm basically out of bread memes. But imagine. Cooked almost exactly.... math years ago. Let's get this bread.
Accurate.
I ended up with three interviews yesterday, but still no specific hiring.
Im down for this kind of pet play, like the car hitting a deer i mentioned yesterday
Oh, this. I don't like this. You take it.
Mood.
Me, waking up from a dream where I was an assistant for someone running for congress who then seduced me and she referred to her newborn baby she brought to functions as a Hummingbird and it was strange and she seduced and married me purely to obtain the re-enacting and wizard votes for her district.
2. It's 2, okay?
When I'm stuck with super sad wanna die moments I keep myself going by reminding myself the next episode of Dinosauria features Dilophosaurus and Scuttelosaurus, two of my favorite dinosaurs, and I don't want to miss it.
Well? Would you?
I love shrikes so much.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "Hi baby!" Today's message from Baja: "What a pretty bird! [Kiss noise]" ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin

item.title: Save This For When You're In Top 1%
item.description: I was explaining to someone how the top 1% can survive on "no income" to report. Here's a good graphic of what I was explaining. The US tax system taxes income, not wealth. Many use a "Buy, Borrow, Die" Strategy: Instead of selling stock to generate cash, wealthy individuals often borrow money using their stock holdings as collateral. This allows them to access cash for expenses or new investments without triggering capital gains taxes, as borrowed money is not considered income. When the individual dies, the assets are passed to heirs who receive a "stepped-up basis", effectively resetting the asset's value for tax purposes and eliminating capital gains taxes for the inheritor. Musk famously used this tactic to help fund his purchase of Twitter, borrowing billions against his Tesla stock. ***Edit: The question of "how is the loan repayed?" is asked in many places. Using the combination of the "Borrow and Die" strategy for the borrower and "Step-Up Basis" for the heir allows the heir to repay the loan and still have a tidy sum. Check that question below for a more detailed response...based on my understanding. Here's a good source.. https://www.dcfpi.org/all/how-wealthy-households-use-a-buy-borrow-die-strategy-to-avoid-taxes-on-their-growing-fortunes/
item.dc:creator: UnattendedDeviant

item.title: Borowitz: "Confused Septuagenarian"
item.description: WASHINGTON (The Borowitz Report)—An unscrupulous Russian man has lured a confused septuagenarian to Alaska in an apparent elder scam, concerned associates of the old man reported on Thursday. According to those associates, the Russian has posed as a friend of his geriatric mark in order to take advantage of him in the remote, icy setting. “This poor, addled codger isn’t playing with a full deck and hasn’t for some time,” one associate said. “We’re afraid that the Russian will trick him into signing something away.” The situation is particularly troubling, the associate said, because “he’s a feeble old man who likes to wander around on top of buildings, and the Russian likes pushing people off them.”
item.dc:creator: JasonM1

item.title: #ListenUp: MOS - "Helloland" (200!)
item.description: MOS are a neo-brass band based out of Osaka, Japan. They've been around since ~2022 and have put out two studio albums with the most recent having been released last year (2024). I think people may know them from the clips (e.g. https://www.youtube.com/shorts/tHNfhDBd5HI) that have gone viral or maybe when they were on America's Got Talent back in 2023 (do people actually watch that?), but I don't think people have actually gone out and listened to their music — which I think is a cryin' shame! They're actually really good and their music might be a fit for people who like other artists like Caravan Palace or similar. Check out the full song here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j1MeFQQF9yI The #ListenUp playlists: - YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL9dLeE7izSbj85xKeSfV6wGl-tsSzgsG3 - YouTube Music: https://music.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL9dLeE7izSbj85xKeSfV6wGl-tsSzgsG3 - Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/playlist/3vHW25MsCYCAylJVVKkJ7O?si=iFrOnK58Se6D-AzKHeVOHQ --- Day 200 (Two hundred days! Two hundred artists!) These #ListenUp posts are my way of hopefully helping a few people discover some new (or forgotten!) music and getting involved with the imgur community. I try to post regularly, so keep an eye open if you're into it. Comment down below if you have an opinion on today's music or if there's one (just one, please!) song you'd recommend I check out. Cheers!
item.dc:creator: sqow

item.title: Three decades later, this man will play one ofthe greatest villains in history.
item.description: Quentin Tarantino nearly walked away from Inglourious Basterds (2009)—not over budget concerns or script troubles, but because he couldn’t find an actor capable of playing Colonel Hans Landa. After searching for seven exhausting months, he began to fear the role was simply “unplayable.” Then Christoph Waltz entered the audition room.
